name: redlipped cleaner wrasse
nifty_facts: Sets up cleaning stations to remove parasites from other fish.

name: reef squirrelfish
nifty_facts: Nocturnal and hides in caves during the day.

name: reticulate boxfish
nifty_facts: Males have a reticulated pattern, while females are more uniform.

name: ring-top cowrie
nifty_facts: Its shell has a distinct yellow-orange ring.

name: rock beauty
nifty_facts: Feeds heavily on sponges.

name: rock flower anemone
nifty_facts: Comes in a wide variety of vibrant colors.
description: orange, white, green, yellow, blue, red, pink, purple, brown

name: royal empress angelfish
nifty_facts: Very difficult to acclimate to aquarium life.

name: rummynose tetra
nifty_facts: Its red nose indicates good water quality.

name: sabre squirrelfish
nifty_facts: Also known as the longjaw squirrelfish.

name: saddleback butterflyfish
nifty_facts: Has a prominent black "saddle" mark on its back.

name: saddleback clownfish
nifty_facts: Known to host in the saddle carpet anemone.

name: sailfin snapper
nifty_facts: Has very long, trailing filaments on its dorsal fin.

name: sailfin tang
nifty_facts: Can expand its fins to look much larger.

name: sand tiger shark
nifty_facts: Gulps air at the surface to maintain buoyancy.

name: sandbar shark
nifty_facts: Has a very tall, triangular first dorsal fin.

name: sapphire damselfish
nifty_facts: Striking bright blue coloration.

name: schoolmaster snapper
nifty_facts: Often forms large resting schools during the day.

name: schwartz's catfish
nifty_facts: Has mild venom in its pectoral and dorsal spines.

name: scrawled cowfish
nifty_facts: Covered in beautiful, scrawled blue lines.

name: scrawled filefish
nifty_facts: Can change its color to blend in with its surroundings.

name: scribbled angelfish
nifty_facts: Often confused with the true personifer angelfish.

name: sea star
nifty_facts: Can regenerate lost arms.

name: sea urchin
nifty_facts: Controls algae populations on coral reefs.

name: semicircle angelfish
nifty_facts: Juveniles have blue and white semicircular stripes.

name: sergeant major
nifty_facts: Males turn bluish and guard the eggs aggressively.

name: sharp-knobbed mud snail
nifty_facts: Often found in very high densities.

name: shorthorn butterflyfish
nifty_facts: Often feeds on soft corals and zooplankton.

name: shortnosed unicorn tang
nifty_facts: Has a short, bony horn on its forehead.

name: shrimpfish
nifty_facts: Swims vertically with its head pointing downwards.

name: sickle butterflyfish
nifty_facts: Has a distinct saddle-like pattern on its back.

name: silver squirrelfish
nifty_facts: Nocturnal and hides in caves during the day.

name: silverspot squirrelfish
nifty_facts: Has a prominent silvery-white spot near its tail.

name: singapore angelfish
nifty_facts: hermaphroditic

name: singular bannerfish
nifty_facts: The largest species of bannerfish.

name: six banded angelfish
nifty_facts: Adults have prominent blue rings.

name: sixbarred angelfish
nifty_facts: Known to grunt loudly when disturbed.

name: sixplate sawtail surgeon
nifty_facts: Has six bony plates on each side of its tail.

name: sixline wrasse
nifty_facts: Great at controlling parasitic snails in aquariums.

name: skunk clowfish
nifty_facts: Has a distinct white stripe running along its back.

name: slate-pencil sea urchin
nifty_facts: Has very thick, blunt spines.

name: smallmouth grunt
nifty_facts: Forms dense schools during the day.

name: smooth star coral
nifty_facts: Can tolerate slightly murky water.

name: snail
nifty_facts: Important part of the cleanup crew in aquariums.

name: soldierfish
nifty_facts: Nocturnal predators with large eyes.

name: solorensis fairy wrasse
nifty_facts: Males display vibrant colors during courtship.

name: southseas devil
nifty_facts: Very aggressive for its small size.

name: spanish hogfish
nifty_facts: Juveniles pick parasites off larger fish.

name: splash-backed poison frog
nifty_facts: Secretes toxins derived from its diet of ants.

name: spottail threadfin snapper
nifty_facts: Often confused with the Sailfin Snapper.

name: spotted croucher
nifty_facts: Lives exclusively among the branches of Acropora corals.

name: spotted mandarin goby
nifty_facts: Secretes a toxic, foul-tasting mucus.

name: spotted prawn goby
nifty_facts: Forms a symbiotic relationship with pistol shrimp.

name: spotted scat
nifty_facts: Can live in freshwater, brackish, and marine environments.

name: spotted tang
nifty_facts: Also known as the Kole Tang.

name: squarespot fairy basslet
nifty_facts: Males have a distinct purple square on their side.

name: squaretail bristletooth
nifty_facts: Uses bristle-like teeth to scrape algae off rocks.

name: squirrelfish
nifty_facts: Produce grunting sounds using their swim bladder.

name: star coral
nifty_facts: Often forms massive, boulder-like colonies.

name: starck's demoiselle
nifty_facts: Deepwater species with striking blue and yellow colors.

name: stars and stripes pufferfish
nifty_facts: Can inflate its body with water to deter predators.

name: stick catfish
nifty_facts: Camouflages itself to look like a submerged twig.

name: stocky basslet
nifty_facts: Males have a distinct red spot on their dorsal fin.

name: stocky pink anthias
nifty_facts: Forms large aggregations over the reef.

name: stony coral
nifty_facts: The primary builders of coral reefs.

name: streaked spinefoot
nifty_facts: Has venomous dorsal, anal, and pelvic spines.

name: striped anemone
nifty_facts: Can reproduce asexually by longitudinal fission.

name: striped anemonefish
nifty_facts: Usually hosts in the bubble-tip anemone.

name: striped bristletooth
nifty_facts: Uses its specialized teeth to scrape algae.

name: striped cleaner wrasse
nifty_facts: Sets up cleaning stations to remove parasites from other fish.

name: striped squirrelfish
nifty_facts: Endemic to Hawaii.

name: sunburst butterflyfish
nifty_facts: Often feeds on soft corals and zooplankton.

name: zebrasoma tang
nifty_facts: Have a sharp scalpel-like spine on their tail.

name: teardrop butterflyfish
nifty_facts: Has a prominent black "teardrop" spot on its side.

name: tetra
natural_hab: Freshwater rivers in South America and Africa
nifty_facts: Many species have a small adipose fin.

name: thompson surgeon
nifty_facts: Feeds primarily on zooplankton rather than algae.

name: threadfin seabass
nifty_facts: Often found in deep water, making them rare in the hobby.

name: three-colored hermit crab
nifty_facts: Very popular in reef aquariums for algae control.

name: threespot angelfish
nifty_facts: Feeds heavily on sponges in the wild.

name: tobaccofish
nifty_facts: Simultaneous hermaphrodite.

name: tomato anemonefish
nifty_facts: Females are much larger and darker than males.

name: tomato clownfish
nifty_facts: Highly territorial, especially when guarding eggs.

name: turban snail
nifty_facts: Excellent at consuming hair algae in aquariums.

name: twinspot goby
nifty_facts: Sifts sand and often forms pairs.

name: two banded anemonefish
natural_hab: Red Sea and Gulf of Aden
nifty_facts: The only anemonefish found in the Red Sea.

name: two bar spinefoot
nifty_facts: Has venomous dorsal spines.

name: two-spot bristletooth
nifty_facts: Has two prominent black spots near its tail.

name: twospot basslet
nifty_facts: Males have distinct red spots on their dorsal fin.

name: twospot hogfish
nifty_facts: One of the smallest hogfish species.

name: vagabond butterflyfish
nifty_facts: Very adaptable and hardy in aquariums.

name: vase coral
nifty_facts: Grows in a distinctive vase or cup shape.

name: wavy turban snail
nifty_facts: Has a beautifully sculpted, wavy shell.

name: white-faced butterflyfish
natural_hab: Red Sea and Gulf of Aden
nifty_facts: Looks very similar to a dwarf angelfish.

name: whitecheek surgeon
nifty_facts: Has a distinct white patch under its eye.

name: whitefreckled surgeon
nifty_facts: Has pale freckles on its head.

name: whitespotted bamboo shark
nifty_facts: A bottom-dwelling shark that rests during the day.

name: whitetail bristletooth
nifty_facts: Its white tail contrasts with its dark body.

name: whitetailed damselfish
nifty_facts: Often lives in branching corals.

name: wounded wrasse
nifty_facts: Named for a red mark that looks like a wound.

name: wrasse
nifty_facts: A very diverse family of brightly colored fish.

name: yellow candy hogfish
nifty_facts: Has a striking yellow and red striped pattern.

name: yellow cleaner wrasse
nifty_facts: Excellent at eating parasitic flatworms.

name: yellow clownfish
nifty_facts: Has a distinct white stripe along its back.

name: yellow longnose butterflyfish
nifty_facts: Has one of the longest snouts of any butterflyfish.

name: yellow stripe angelfish
nifty_facts: Secretes ostracitoxin when stressed.

name: yellow tang
nifty_facts: One of the most popular marine aquarium fish.

name: yellow-and-blueback fusilier
nifty_facts: Often forms large schools in open water.

name: yellow-banded poison dart frog
nifty_facts: The only poison dart frog known to aestivate during dry spells.

name: yellowback goatfish
nifty_facts: Uses its barbels to hunt for prey in the sand.

name: yellowbanded angelfish
nifty_facts: Has a distinct yellow map-like mark on its side.

name: yellowfaced angelfish
nifty_facts: Has a striking yellow mask over its eyes.

name: yellowfin surgeon
nifty_facts: One of the largest species of surgeonfish.

name: zebra angelfish
nifty_facts: Males have distinct zebra-like stripes.

name: zebra nerite snail
nifty_facts: Excellent algae eater that won't reproduce in freshwater.

name: zebra shark
nifty_facts: Juveniles have zebra stripes, while adults have leopard spots.

name: caribbean spiny lobster
nifty_facts: Lacks large claws and uses long spiny antennae for defense.
